title: E-Yooso 60% Retro Steampunk Mechanical Gaming Keyboard, Luxury Vintage USB Keyboard with Compact 81 Key RGB Backlit, Linear Red Switch
description:
★ Specifications
Keyboard type: Retro Wired Mechanical Keyboard
Base Material: Aluminum Alloy
Keycap Material: Double Shot Injection
Interface: USB in Golden Plating
Backlight: RGB
Switches: Gaote/Outemu Red- Cherry MX Red Switch Similar
Key lifetime: Over 50 million clicks
Key Profile: 4.0+/-0.2MM
Detachable Cable Length: 1.5 meters
Keyboard Size: L 12.1 x W 4.7 x H 1.35 in
Keyboard Weight: About 740g/1.63lb
❤ Package Contents:
1x Retro Keyboard
1x Keycap Remover

5 x Replaceable Outemu Red Switch
features: Hot Swappable Outemu RED Switches ❤ Red switches offer much linear and quiet Sound when you type or play games so that there is less clicking noise, it will be fit at home or office. And you can even displace different switches, such as Blue Switch, Brown Switches or Speed Switches etc
Retro Round Keycaps for Typewriter ❤ Genuine & Luxurious appearance, Classic Typewriting craftsmanship Keycaps, and exclusiveness while emitting a unique charm
21+ RGB Backlit Themes ❤ 14 lighting modes by Pressing FN+PgDn, 7 solid light modes via FN+-/=. You will love the keyboard world with Charming Lighting when home work or office business.
81 Key Anti-Ghosting Gaming Keyboard ❤ Supporting super fast input that makes it even suitable for gaming competition. Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard 60% saves much space for your right hand.
Wide Compatibility ❤ Plug and play, no drivers or software are required. Supported computer systems, Windows 10, Windows 8, Windows 7, Windows XP, Windows Vista and Mac OS

reviews.title: Spacebar sticks occasionally.
reviews.text: The space bar sticks sometimes, but the actuation for is suitable for my sore index finger from using my mouse a lot. I am a smaller person, my hands fall between medium and small. I wanted something with a smaller key distance and slightly smaller keys for typing and this definitely fits the bill. It comes with some replacement switches, we'll see how long it takes to actually need to the switch.

reviews.title: I LOVE it!
reviews.text: Looks amazing in my set up and love the retro typewriter feel.

reviews.title: Hubby loves it!
reviews.text: So I initially got this for my parents, I mistakenly thought it was a wireless keyboard. IT IS NOT A WIRELESS KEYBOARD. However, I intended to take it back until my husband got a hold of it and loved the lights and the different patterns that can be done as well as the old fashioned typing clicks.

reviews.title: it doesn't make a clickety clakety sound like the old IBM keyboards, which I thought it would
reviews.text: I gave it to one of my grandkids, after buying a wrist rest for it. So I used it only once.

reviews.title: ❤🧡💛💚💙💜
reviews.text: Smaller than expected but it's very pretty and the keys work great

reviews.title: As expected
reviews.text: I was a bit wary because it's dye sub but the printing was perfect and came in exactly 2 weeks from China. Definitely better than the Akko EVA especially since the color on the Akko's are very noticeably lighter then pictures. I returned them the next day

reviews.title: Good quality, thick keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my case perfectly. Feels nice and looks nice.

reviews.title: Good quality and adorable!
reviews.text:
Key caps are textured, but do not feel rough. The textured PBT will keep them from getting shiny as fast (compared to smooth ABS). No print errors. Fit the Cherry switches perfectly. They feel weighty and durable. The plastic is cut cleanly, no rough edges. Nice big font, centered on each key. I am very happy with these.

The only thing I am a bit disappointed about is that the special (non-standard) Capslock key does not fit my keyboard; the slot where you are supposed to insert the switch is not centered correctly. But, it isn't enough of a disappointment for me to knock down my rating.

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: These keycaps are made of PBT, which is about the highest grade of plastic that you can get for your keycaps. I love the flatter profile of the XDA keycaps. The print on the keys is very classic, large, and easy to read. This keycap set contains both peach and sea green keycaps for the function keys, space bar, escape, and enter keys. You can mix and match them until your hearts delight. You even get some extra keys for media keys if your keyboard has them, plus menu and function keys. You get a wide variety of keys that will fit most Cherry MX style keyboards easily. The plastic is nice and textured so your fingers won't slip off the keycaps when you are typing on the keyboard. I would highly recommend these keycaps as an upgrade to ABS keycaps, and for customizing your keyboard in a cute vintage style.

reviews.title: It’s so fricken cute
reviews.text: It’s not bad quality. Keyboard RGB does shine through fairly well but looks a little odd especially if you mix it in with PBT Puddings. The paint work is really good quality for how little they’re asking. The same thing can be found on other sites but more expensive than it on Amazon. Genuine 10/10 from me and highly recommend it.

reviews.title: So cute!
reviews.text: Reminds me of my cat and he is a bit tall but it’s fine with me! So adorable and worth it

reviews.title: Key cap
reviews.text: Bought for my granddaughter and she loves it

title: dagaladoo XVX 189 Keys Double Shot Keycaps, PBT Custom Keyboard Keycaps Full Set, XVX Profile Keycaps for 60% 65% 70% 100% Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Black/Purple
features: Full 189 keys PBT keycaps set, including 108 standard keys, 61 supplementary keys and 20 invert color supplementary keys for most 61/64/66/68/84/87/96/98/100/104/108 mechanical keyboards.
Double shot design keycaps, made from two colors material molded into each others, make the keycaps characters maintain the vibrance and saturation, clear and not fade.
XVX profile keycaps, ergonomic height design similar than Cherry profile, while increasing the contact surface between the finger and keycaps, bring you a comfortable typing experience.
Made from high content PBT material, 0.2cm extra-thick keycaps wall, which is provide top-notch durability, excellent touch and grease resistance.
Minimalist theme style and classic color matching, with a large number of personalized complement keys, you can DIY different styles of keyboards according to your own ideas.

reviews.title: Fits Royal Kludge 90% and Keychron K10!
reviews.text: I like all the extras. The quality of the PBT is grainy in texture, so if that's the kind of thing you're in to, then these won't disappoint. Not shine through, although the advertisement never claims they are. No light is coming through these from your board. The XVX profile is nice. Many extra key options- this set has fit several boards from several brands- not something you see for the price point! Quality printing and nice font on the caps. The keys feel good to type on when used with a clicky or linear switch. They are a bit heavy, so a lighter switch wouldn't be a bad option. I don't like to use these on tactile switches due to the mushy that these caps add to the keys. O Rings did not help these keys IMO.

reviews.title: Random assortment of loose keys
reviews.text: Expected a full set of keycaps, instead received a box containing three bags of loose keys, missing multiple letters, numbers, function keys, etc. Might be good for a concept art project, but not for a working keyboard.

reviews.title: Some keys misaligned with switch stems.
reviews.text: The keys have a nice touch, sound and printing however some keys, particularly my "O" key are a misaligned with the switch stem causing it to stick up a little higher than the "I" and "P" on either side. I did not mistake a "0" for an "O".

reviews.title: Seem to be good quality, but I hate the XVX profile
reviews.text:
These seems to be good quality keycaps, I have no issues with them at all. I have put them on multiple boards now, and I haven't had any break or scratch. Colors are accurate and legends are clear. No complaints there.

My one gripe is the XVX profile. It takes a lot of getting used to (like any new profile would), but the surface area of the keys is much larger than most other profiles. Therefore, when typing, it feels as if your fingers have to travel significantly further to reach the next key. It also makes it much, much easier to fat finger extra keys. If I had realized how much more surface area there was, I would not have purchased these. However, they do look great, they were cheap, and they sound pretty good too.

reviews.title: Great keycaps, andd even better customer service!
reviews.text: I wanted to find some PBT keycaps that were double-shot and gave some aesthetic flair to my $15 108 full sized mech keyboard with brown switches.  They fit just fine, and give a different, yet, pleasing sounds and feel to the keyboard.  I wanted non-see through keycaps in order to hide most of the LED rainbow glare.  The backlit effect is, again, very pleasing.  The keycap set that I originally ordered was missing the F9 key.  I contacted their customer service through Amazon, and got a reply back right away and arranged for a replacement set to be shipped out.  Got it the new set the day after, and it was complete.  Will purchase from this seller again whenever I need/want new keycaps.  Very confident if something is wrong, will be taken care of in a timely matter.

title: Kraken Coiled Keyboard Cable - Artisan Coiled USB C Cable with Aviator Connector for Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (Pure White)
features: Premium Artisan Designs in colors to fit every gaming setup!
Heavy Duty 5 Pin Aviator Connector (Easy to connect, and looks AMAZING)
Double Sleeved for enhanced looks and durability!
5 Feet long to reach your keyboard anywhere in your setup

reviews.title: Excellent quality cable and connectors, but color is more purple than blue
reviews.text:
Update: Oddly enough, this did not work with a Drop Alt keyboard. It worked with a few other keyboards just fine though. Just a word of warning for anyone pairing this cable with a Drop Alt (or maybe any other Drop keyboard).

This is the third coiled cable I have ordered, and it's definitely the best quality overall. The cable itself is thick and good quality. The coil is a bit firm, but nothing too unusual. The intermediate connector is all metal and heavy. Both sides of the intermediate connector can be unthreaded: The side going to the USB-C connector, and the side going to the USB connector.

One interesting thing I noticed was that the end connectors have a color matched almost shrink wrap thickness strain protection around them. The USB side is the most noticeable, and the USB-C side is a bit thicker. As long as there is enough strain protection, I actually prefer the less bulky end connections.

The only nit is the color. Even though it looked dark purplish in the product listing, I was hoping that it would be more blue in real life. It's not, it's an attractive dark purple. I would assume the color represented in the product listing is accurate, in case you're wondering if there is any color variation from the listing photos to real life.

title: Hyekit Custom Gaming Keyboard - Alice Layout 66 Keys - Support QMK/Via Programmable Key - Gasket Structure - USB C Wired Mechanical Keyboard Kit - RGB Hot Swappable Switches & Keycaps - Acrylic Shell
description:
Note:
This link only sells a kit, not a finished keyboard, you need to assemble it yourself, the kit does not include switches, keycaps, data cables, or stabilizers.
About This Item
1.FancyAlice66 keyboard kit Alice is equipped with 66 keys, ergonomic, yes typing is more comfortable.  2. Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches.  3. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, kit supports QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions.  4. South-facing RGB Backlit has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight makes the keyboard look more attractive.  5.Gasket Mount, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, which makes the keyboard more comfortable and soft to type, and the typing sound is more pleasant.  6. Acrylic stacking shell, the texture is very good, enjoy the fun of custom assembling the shell.  7. Wired mode, support USB-C, typing response is faster, Type-c interface adapts to more data lines.  8. The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. First, ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly.  9.Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers.
Package includes:
Keyboard case*1  PCB*1  Quiet sandwich cotton*1  Switch pad*1  Assemble some parts
features: 【Package Included】Keyboard case*1, PCB*1, Quiet sandwich cotton*1, Switch pad*1, Assemble some parts. No USB cables, switches, stabilizer, and keycaps. (Note, The kit needs to be assembled by yourself. you ensure that you have a certain ability to assemble. You can refer to our assembly instruction video for assembly. Let's enjoy the process of assembling the keyboard and the great sense of achievement brought by the successful assembly.)

【Custom Gaming Keyboard】Hot-swappable PCB uses Kailh hot-swappable socket and supports 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches. Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions. Wired mode, supports USB-C, typing response is faster.

【Alice Layout】The Alice layout considers the natural input angle of the wrist from the perspective of ergonomics. When using the conventional arrangement keyboard for continuous high-intensity input work, the unnatural bending of the wrist will easily lead to hand fatigue, and the Alice layout can solve this problem very well, it takes into account the integrity of the keyboard and the relatively relaxed input state of the wrist.

【South-facing RGB Backlit】Alice66 has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight, the RBG Backlight effect is very bright even in the daytime. The lower lamp position is designed so that the light will not be blocked by the switch. The fully transparent bottom cover allows the 16 bottom lights to display their backlight effects without obstruction, making the keyboard look more attractive.

【Acrylic Gasket Structure】Alice66 is designed with Gasket structure, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, the sound is pure, and the feel is soft. This wonderful feel can make the typing process very pleasant and full of fun.

reviews.title: Sounds great with no mods
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:58cc2520e806ff1428542a1df7456920]] Assembly was pretty easy for this DIY kit. It even sounds great without any modifications. I decided to put poron switch pads on the pcb since the foam it came with did not line up properly. The feet that comes with this kit is too aggressive for my liking. I decided to remove it and put clear rubber bumpons

Did not come with stabilizers. I was hoping it does at this price point. Speaking of which, this kit can be had for cheaper on other retailers. But ordering from Amazon gave me more confidence.

My copy came with cracked and scuffed case. I messaged the seller directly and they were able to help and send me a new case promptly. They also helped with VIA setup on this keyboard.

Pros:
- easy assembly
- VIA support PCB
- great sound with little to no modifications
- south facing, per key RGB
- under glow lighting
- A+ great customer support from seller

Cons:
- price seems higher compared to other websites
- inconsistent craftsmanship
- no stabilizers included
- front of the keyboard can be wobbly with the aggressive feet riser installed

(Keycaps: MT3 2048; Switches: WS Aurora Clears)

title: TECWARE Phantom 87 Key Mechanical Keyboard, RGB led, Outemu Brown Switch
features: RGB BACKLIGHTING - Enhance your gaming experience with 18 pre-set configurations on the mechanical keyboard, or create a unique one to match your setup

OUTEMU SWITCHES - The gaming mechanical keyboard comes with Outemu mechanical switches for precise gaming and comfortable typing. Spare switches + keycap & switch remover tools are provided for easy replacement
FIBERGLASS PCB - Built with SMD LEDs and FR-4 fiberglass printed circuit board, this Tecware mechanical keyboard is able to withstand extended periods of intensive and hardcore gaming sessions

DOUBLE-SHOT KEYCAPS – Featuring an all-new font, the keycaps on this mechanical keyboard are double-shot ABS (dual-injection molding process) which guarantee high resistance against surface wear; markings that cannot be worn off as they are physically part of the keycaps

SPEED ADVANTAGE - With full N-key rollover and 1000Hz pooling rate, Tecware keyboards allow faster and smoother gaming experience. The depression of each key is detected individually, meaning all key presses will be registered, no matter how many keys you press down simultaneously

reviews.title: Great quality
reviews.text: Very bright lights. Click clack noise keys. Small the way I like it. Portable. Works great. Has usb cover cap too.

reviews.title: Excellent keyboard.
reviews.text: This is my first mechanical keyboard and I really love the quality of this keyboard. The LED's are bright and on keyboard LED controls are very useful as you don't have to depend on the software. The keyboard feels very premium and on par with any other expensive keyboard. I would highly recommend this to others looking for new mechanical keyboard.

reviews.title: the smaller one with the browns... i love it
reviews.text: ok the onboard RGB stuff is really why i bought this. Its not Razer RGB but for +/- 50 bucks? oh hell yea. The keys are great, if your used to others it might take a sec to get accustomed, but if you use it your fingers will get to know it real nice :) Solid thru and thru.

reviews.title: Yes
reviews.text: Custom color mapping, might need to buy some more resistant switches for keys near my palms.. or I could learn to type properly. Either way, I approve.

reviews.title: Great colors
reviews.text: Exactly what my son wanted.  Color control is amazing.

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

reviews.title: Great Authentic Cherry Switches
reviews.text: Great authentic Cherry switches. I'm writing this review on them now. They come in a zip loc bag and a few had bent pins. That is to be expected, I always order more than I actually need for this very reason.

title: Lomiluskr K680T Wireless 60% Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Compact Bluetooth/Wired Dual Mode Keyboard, White Backlight, Compatible for Multi-Device Connection (Black Switch, Black)
features: 【68 Key Mini and Compact Layout】60% Layout 68Keys Mechanical Keyboard. As a compact mechanical keyboard, while minimize the size to 13.4×4.25×1.4inch, we maintain the Mac key position and commonly used keys for games. This size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space, whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Small in the appearance but big inside

【Bluetooth Wireless/Wired Mode】Designed with Bluetooth mode and wired dual mode, you have more choices. Bluetooth3.0 is stable and strong enough to ensure 10 meters transmission distance. It can connect up to 3 devices at the same time, simply press FN+Q/W/E to exchange between devices, easy to use and suitable for both work and game. Moreover, the wired mode will also charge the keyboard at the same time, a cool function and time-saver indeed

【1000mAh Built-in Large Capacity Battery】N-Key Rollover and Built-in 1000mAh Battery. This model is programmed to be N-key rollover in wired mode. The keys won’t conflict with each other. Save you from trouble while playing a game. Built-in large capacity battery ensures long time use without falling behind

【18 Light Effects】The letters are made to shine-through if the backlight is on. Simple backlight but you have got up to 18 light effects, classic and neat (flashing, static or breathing, etc.) use FN+→/←/↑/↓ to choose the ideal mode, freely adjust the brightness and light speed.

【Professional Mechanical Switches】Mechanical Switches and Professional Chip. This model is equipped with the custom chip for fast and lossless response. The 68 keys are using the real mechanical switches for ultimate tapping experience. Lightweight, weak tactile and audible feedback, these switches are the perfect combination of typing and gaming, more than 50 million click tests for excellent durability.

reviews.title: Won't pair with M1 Macbook Air, works well with Dell XPS 9370 and Acer Triton Predator
reviews.text:
For windows users this keyboard is great. The brown switches feel somewhat similar to higher end cherry brown switches and I had no complaints with the build quality. There are various lighting modes (my favorite is the single keystroke light trail).

I was slightly disappointed with the Bluetooth not working on my M1 Macbook Air but I had no other issues with various windows laptops. There is some latency noticeable when waking from sleep but it's not too big of a deal. For gamers it might be though so you'll want to use it wired.

Overall it's a good deal for a mechanical 60% bt keyboard as long as you're not expecting premium switches or bt 5.0 connectivity.

reviews.title: Compact But Has Good Weight, Keys Are Perfect
reviews.text:
I opted for the "brown" keys and I must say that they feel and respond perfectly. Just the right amount of "clack" and just the right amount of resistance. The keys are normal width but the footprint is compact so you don't have to sacrifice any typing comfort for the portability or the ability to work in small spaces. The keyboard itself is very weighty and between its heft and rubber feet it's pleasingly very stable - won't slide around on the desk. The ability to use either USB or Bluetooth makes this thing incredibly versatile. Thankfully it's USB-C so you don't have to worry about plug alignment, and the included USB cable is very good quality (braided nylon, strain relief) and length. The backlighting is decent, though not terribly even so some of the lettering doesn't look right in the dark.

One big downside is the lack of dedicated Function keys (F1-F12). This keyboard takes a cue from laptop keyboard design and introduces an "Fn" key which you have to hold in to change the function of normal keys to specialty operations, but instead of controlling volume or screen brightness, the "Fn" key is required to activate bluetooth pairing, an feature that acts sort of like Caps Lock but for function keys and of course the "F" keys. The problem I ran into with this configuration is that the F1-F12 keys weren't being recognized with some devices. I actually bought this specifically to use with a MiSTer FPGA device which supports standard USB keyboards and requires the F keys for certain basic functions.

According to the instructions, the combination "Fn + 1" should look like an "F1" key press but it did not. After poking at it for some time I discovered an undocumented (as in it's not in the instructions anywhere) feature which subtly changes the keyboard's operation. The "A" and "S" keys have an additional label "Os1" and "Os2" respectively. Holding "Fn + A" or "Fn + S" makes the keyboard backlight flash (which it does whenever you've changed some function of the keyboard itself). With the keyboard set to "Os2" (Fn + S) the "Fn + 1" combination DOES send "F1" but, this arrangement also confusingly swaps the "Window" key and the left "Alt" key.

Bottom Line ---
Pros:
- Heavy
- Quality keys, great feel
- Bluetooth & USB
- USB is USB-C and comes with a good cord
- Easy to plug in and use

Neutral Observations:
- Backlight is uneven

Cons:
- Instructions are incomplete
- Not having dedicated F1-F12 keys is problematic for gaming

title: GK GAMAKAY LK67 65% RGB Mechanical Keyboard with Knob Control, 67 Keys Hot Swap Bluetooth/USB-C Wired/2.4GHz Wireless PBT XDA Profile Keycaps Blue Gaming Keyboard for Win Mac (Gateron Blue Switch)
features: 【LK67 Blue Exclusive Keyboard】LK67 will give you the shot to make a refreshing blue exclusive keyboard. Our keyboard is PBT XDA profile color matching keycaps. Different from ABS keycaps, these PBT keycaps are made from quality material and hold up well over time. PBT is an excellent choice for most gaming setups and it is the most durable material used in keycap manufacturing. PBT is harder and produces a more tactile sound. The durable and amazing touch feeling will definitlly your best companion!

【Triple Mode Connection】Our keyboard supports triple mode connection (3 bluetooth devices/ 1 2.4GHz device/ 1 wired devices), it comes with a detachable USB type-c cable and a 2.4GHz receiver.【Multi-function Knob】Knob in the top right-hand corner is multi-function with one button: Volume+ / Volume- / Play / Stop. Let you achieve more gameplay and more convenient operations.

【RGB Backlight】With automotive grating design and side light RGB lighing effects, to offer immersing gaming experience to you. Each LED has been precisely calibrated to show the perfect RGB Backlit. The keyboard is not only practical, but also the perfect desktop decorations. 【Fully Programmable】Various preset and custom modes, endless shortcuts are all stand by for easy setting and assignment in software, type and play games in your most familiar way.

【50 Million Gateron Switch】 Yellow / Silver / Red / Blue / Black / Brown Switch is available. Equipped with hot swappable terminals, you could install the MX switches directly without soldering, easily build up your own mechanical keyboard. Support NKRO. Supports both 3 pin or 5 pin switch. In addition, the keyboard has a built-in 2400mAh lithium battery.

【65% Compact 67 Keys Keyboard】67 keys compact keyboard make use of each space, this size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space. Whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Note: For the sake of the beauty of the product, the reserved clearance of the keyboard is small, manual disassembly may cause the accessories to fall off or damage.

reviews.title: Nice mechanical keyboard for the money
reviews.text: I have a number of mechanical keyboards that are mostly used for business purposes. My primary motivator for buying this keyboard was to try out the GamaKay Phoenix switches, but I also like the look and size of the keyboard. The price is also really good, in my opinion. Right now this keyboard is the one I reach for the most. I really like the quiet thump of the switches and they feel nice to use, but to be clear - they aren't actually silent. I've had a few annoyances with this keyboard occasionally not "waking up". The lights will respond, but typing doesn't show up. It's an easy fix. I just switch the input type over to BT and then back to USB since I've been using it wired. It immediately starts responding again. This isn't a consistent issue, but is a small thing that I wish didn't happen.

reviews.title: Support is impressive
reviews.text: Great keyboard back up by great service.

reviews.title: Easy to pair; good customizability
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:4822e6b7aa201be2a20c1d13ef6c2077]] Pros:

- Compact. Good mechanical alternative to Magic keyboard if size is an important factor for you.
- Customizable. Hotswappable switches and caps give you a good platform for changes later.

Cons:

- Stock caps are very plasticky and not the best I've used. Print quality and shape are good, but the material makes them feel hollow and gives an odd echo to each keypress. Will be replacing.
- No risers. The flat angle of the keyboard is not comfortable. Needs some sort of elevation in the back.

Meh:

-Stock switches are not the best I've used, not the worst. The yellow gateron's are among my favorite switches. They have a great overall feel. Some keys, particularly spacebar, have some "springy" sound to them and need lubrication or cushioning. Fortunately, the switches are swappable, and this can be adjusted if you're down for further investment and/or a project.
- Bluetooth connects well, but is sometimes laggy on input. Not a huge deal for regular typing, but I wouldn't game on it.
- LED's are always "moving." There's no way to set it to one color and leave it, which is my preferred color set.

Bottom line:

This keyboard is a decent platform for customization and you can MAKE this keyboard into exactly what you want, but I think it's overpriced if that's what you're getting it for.

reviews.title: My favorite keyboard!
reviews.text: I own quite a few of these multi-mode mechanical keyboards, and this is my favorite one by far. The build quality is phenomenal. PBT plastic keycaps with an XDA profile, dual flip out feet, the small layout WITH dedicated arrow keys, and even RGB backlighting. The keyboard uses a USB-C connection for wired mode. You also get a 2.4ghz dongle for wireless connectivity if you don't have Bluetooth. If you do, then you can connect and swap between 3 different Bluetooth devices. The ON/OFF switch and the connection mode switch are on the left side of the keyboard as opposed to the bottom. The RGB backlighting shines around the keycaps but not through them as PBT does not play well with allowing backlighting through a key as it is firmer and heavier than ABS. Still, with the white case cover the lighting looks great. You can also turn off backlighting to extend the battery life of the built-in battery. If you download the driver software, you can easily change the backlighting options without having to worry about remembering too many key combinations. This keyboard looks good and feels better. I got the option with the silver speed switches, and it is a dream with this keyboard. The speed of the actuation plus the feel of the solid PBT makes this keyboard the most comfortable that I own. This keyboard is now my daily driver, even above my HHKB. I highly recommend you try this keyboard. If you want a reduced layout with solid construction and fast response, you cannot get better than this anywhere.

reviews.title: Check Your Computer First
reviews.text:
I sure hated to send this beauty back to Amazon, but I gave it a fair trial and I'm just not satisfied, and here's why.
It's a beautiful piece of machinery/artwork and weighs in at about 10 lbs. The keyboard glows in such a way that it will illuminate your work area in a dark room, and you can adjust the brightness up or down or off.
No, my complaint is with the speed at which it reacts. This keyboard has to keep up with you, possibly in order to mimic an old fashioned Royal or Underwood. I'm used to a $30 Logitech wireless that I can wag to the kitchen with my laptop. I don't like to use the laptop keyboard and scroll pad, I like a separate keyboard, it's faster for me. I find myself correcting errors constantly, and it slows me down, but I'm no stenographer either.
This Azio is Bluetooth, but not all computers support it. My 6 year old Toshiba doesn't, so I have to use the USB connection cord and block a precious port full time.
What a shame, it sure is elegant, and a lot of folks won't care about the perceived lag time.
The star rating posed a problem for me because I'm trying to be honest about my needs and I realize another might be perfectly satisfied.
This brute is expensive, and I'm not inclined to spend north of $700 for a new computer when I don't need one, just to support it.

reviews.title: Good keyboard with one minor catch
reviews.text: Very good keyboard, but it is heavier than I thought.  The height is not easier adjusted so I have to leave my drop down keyboard tray stuck out.

reviews.title: Great Keyboard - Bad Reliability Plus Non-Existent Support
reviews.text: Ordered this roughly a year ago. Started having problems with the mouse within a few months, with the mouse not holding a charge. Got the product replaced under warranty. Replacement mouse never would connect via the USB dongle consistently. 10 months in or so, my keyboard started acting up and, despite being plugged in via USB-C on my desk, continually shows out of power / red light. When I move it to Bluetooth it strangely works, so it can't be out of power. Reached out to Azio support and got crickets. Now have a nearly $200 set of pretty chrome and leather and plastic paperweight. Looks great, fabulous when it work. Sadly that's not very consistently or long. I've got cheap keyboards that lasted multiple years easily.

reviews.title: Not Bad
reviews.text: The keyboard and mouse was packed well for the shipping and arrived in great shape.  Very tactile feel and old school look.

This is a lot of money for a keyboard that has to be plugged in.  Also, I can’t tell how to get in touch with the seller so I can get confirmation.  RETURNING.

reviews.title: I liked it very much until it started disconnecting
reviews.text:
I've only had it for a week and the type C end moves and it disconnects from my keyboard. it's not a lot but enough to power off my key board.

But, aside from that little piece of failure, i really like the sturdiness and the quality of the work on it. It's also at a great price. That's why i'm not returning this item, it's actually really good and as long as the cable is held in place it works great. Maybe it's my WFH wonky setup. I would buy more if i needed more cables, but I'm set for life with cables.

really nice cable, i would recommend to others.

reviews.title: Works great
reviews.text: Purchased for my son.  He says it works great.

reviews.title: Great upgrade for any Keyboard, best with custom rugged builds.
reviews.text: I wanted to get a substitute USB-C cable for my keyboard and found this listing. Was intrigued by the interchangeable inputs as well as the mechanical design. The price seemed steep, but the uses, ergonomics and possible durability of the cable seemed like a good investment. Mine came functional with an included "Kraken Keybaord" sticker, thank you! The only problem I found was that the USB-C male input was a tad long for my ANNE PRO Clone, a Dierya x Kemove DK61 60% Keyboard. It might be my keyboard's own build quality, or that maybe these cables are meant for more rugged custom boards. If I move my keyboard drastically it might disconnect, but luckily that rarely happens. I'm more than satisfied with the cable, more like enamored. I'd buy again for a future custom keyboard build for sure.

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: Impressed
reviews.text: Very good packaging and acceptable quality

reviews.title: Unique keys look cool with RGB. Some drawbacks.
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Beautifully and conveniently packaged and organized. Caps come on three trays with plastic film on them so that nothing will have shifted in transit. It's very easy to replace your caps and keep your old set save and organized in the trays, too. Simple to find any specialized keys that you may need in the trays.
- Print is easy to see and seems durable/high contrast. No funky fonts that are hard to read.
- Looks awesome with RGB.

Cons:

- There are no tactile nubs on the F and J keys to indicate homerow. As a touch typist, this was almost a dealbreaker.
- Color is way off from the images in the description. The images in the description show a clear white color to the keycaps. Along with the "clear/translucent" descriptors, I believed I was going to get more of a white/iced translucent look. Nope. The caps are basically colorless clear. They are smooth and glossy with no texture. I left some of the OG white keys on my keyboard to show the color clearly side by side. Not the look I was expecting from the images and description.

Good to know:

- I've never used ASA profile before, and generally prefer XDA, so these are a little taller than I'm used to on XDA, but more rounded (and, I think, more comfortable, than OEM. I had some lousy stock OEM caps on my old keyboard, and I do feel these caps are an improvement. They are sufficiently dense, and give me a slightly deeper, more muted sound from my keypresses with my brown switches, which I like, compared to the more plasticky/hollow caps that were original to my board.
- Despite having a glossy finish, I don't find these keys to be slippery--the slight curvature leads your fingertips toward the center of the keys easily and they're comfortable to use.
- I really hate that there are no homerow nubs. Who skips that? Come on.

reviews.title: No tactile bumps on F and J, amazing for backlighting
reviews.text:
I have the “White” color option in this set of keycaps from Akko. White is a bit misleading, the color is actually glossy and nearly translucent. This is actually what I wanted, I was looking all over for nearly clear keycaps, but I did want to point that out.

If you like maximum backlighting, this might be the set for you. I was using white pudding caps before, and I thought they let a lot of light shine through. The difference is night and day. These Akko caps show backlighting on all sides and the top of the cap. I’ve included a picture of a white pudding cap surrounded by the clear Akkos to show the difference.

I’m using the keycaps with Akko Vintage White (MX) switches. The keycaps fit well, but they took a bit of force to fit on the switches.

I am disappointed the F and J keys do not have ridges or bumps. I’m a touch typist and using an ergo board with 56 keys, I rely on those ridges to help me orient my hands. This set has so much variety that I wish Akko had included doubles of F and J or even blanks with the ridges.

Speaking of keycap variety: the set has some great 1u options! My current keyboard is an Iris Rev 6, all keys are 1u size. But most keycap sets don’t have 1u keycaps for Shift, Control, Command/Windows, and so on. This set does, which is a big help. I also like the cute animal keycaps, they add some fun to my board.

I believe the ASA height is original to Akko. They have a helpful height chart on their site, comparing ASA to OEM, JDA, OSA, and Cherry. I was using OEM before, and switching to ASA and the glossy feel of the keycaps took about two days of adjustment.

title: Glorious Aura V2 (Black) - PBT Pudding Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards - ANSI (US), ISO Compatible - Supports Full Size, TKL, 75%, 60% Layouts

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

reviews.title: Legend doesn’t glow like other pics
reviews.text: For my Tofu65, I do like these pudding key caps, but I’m not sure why the legends on my key caps aren’t transparent. Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

title: YUNZII AKKO CS Switch, 3 Pins for DIY MX Mechanical Keyboard, 45 pcs (Ocean Blue Switch)
features: 【About AKKO CS Switches】 AKKO Custom Series (CS) switches are the newly designed 3-pin switches for customers who have specific typing and gaming needs. Each package has 45 switches. Six types of switches are available and no matter which kind of typing feeling you want, we cover it all.

【Compatibility】AKKO CS switches are designed with a general MX structure so that they can fit most keycaps sets. The switch has 3 pins but also fit in all 3 pins and 5 pins keyboards. Based on the strong compatibility, you can easily change the switch and customize your mechanical keyboard.
【Built-in LED Slot 】The AKKO CS switches are equipped with a north-facing LED slot for LED modification. It is also compatible with SMD LED, provides more fun and options for keyboard enthusiasts.

【Durable Lifespan & Improved Performance】With a lifespan of approximately 50 million times of keystrokes, you are assured of high quality and a long lifespan. The CS switches feature a spiral spring design that can make the typing more comfortable.

【Ocean Blue Tactile Switch】The tactile bump of the Ocean Blue switch is at 0.55mm and it ends at 1.88mm, which gives long-continued feedback. Different from the blue and brown switch, the Ocean blue switch has a medium tactile position that can give you an excellent experience in typing and gaming.

reviews.title: Very good for the price
reviews.text: I'm not a mechanical keyboard connoisseur, but needed to replace the switches on my cheap Redragon keyboard to get rid of the loud clicking noise. The original switches were blue switch clones and the clicks will wake up the neighbors. These are cheap enough that I decided to give them a try. They are pretty good in terms of touch and sound, from my perspective and my use. Will definitely resort to these again in the future if I need to replace keyboard switches, especially for budget keyboards.
